sutta: once when the exalted 1 was staying near raja gaha on mount valchar pete sutawan the wanderer came and paid him a visit after greeting the exalted 1 and exchanging the usual complementary speeches he sat down at 1 side so seated he addressed the exalted wonders now on 1 occasion lord when the exalted 1 was staying here at rajya gaha on giri baja i heard this from the mouth of the exalted 1 from his own mouth i learned it the arahant monk sutawan who has destroyed the asavas lived the holy life done what was to be done laid down the burden 1 to the goal burst the bonds of becoming and is freed by the fullness of knowledge cannot transgress the 5 standards among in whom the asavas are destroyed cannot deliberately take the life of any living thing cannot with intention to steal take what is not given cannot indulge in carnal intercourse cannot intentionally tell a lie cannot enjoy pleasures from savings as of before when a householder pray lord have i heard the matter rightly from the exalted 1 have i grasped it rightly have i borne it in mind rightly have i understood it rightly and the buddha said certainly sutawan you have heard the matter rightly grasp it rightly born it in mind brightly and understood it rightly both in the past and now sutaban i verily declare the arahant monk who has destroyed the asavas lived the holy life done what was to be done lay down the burden 1 to the goal burst the bonds of becoming and is freed by the fullness of knowledge cannot transgress 9 standards among in whom the asavas are destroyed cannot deliberately take the life of any living thing cannot with intention to steal take what is not given cannot indulge in carnal intercourse cannot intentionally tell a lie cannot enjoy pleasures from savings as of before when a householder among in whom the asavas are destroyed cannot go astray true desire cannot go astray through hate cannot go astray true delusion it cannot go astray through fear both in the past and now superwoman i verily declare an arahant monk who has lived the life the holy life done what was to be done laid down the burden 1 to the goal burst the bonds of becoming and is freed by the fullness of knowledge cannot transgress these 9 standards this is a quite an interesting suta here the buddha is saying that um an enlightened person cannot break these 9 standards 1 he cannot intentionally kill 2 he cannot intentionally steal 3 cannot indulge in sexual intercourse number 4 cannot tell a lie number 5 cannot save up something to use [Music] um later in other words something uh food or something because he's not worried about whether he lives or not and then after that he cannot do something wrong because of desire or greed he cannot do something wrong because of hatred he cannot do something wrong because of delusion and he cannot do something wrong because of fear so these are the 9 standards to judge arahan and so this is from the earlier suitas but sometimes if you're not careful you'll read some later books they may say something which is contrary to this for example they may claim that such and such a person is enlightened yet he can break these precepts then he cannot be an enlightened person if that is so nexuta is
